reports.title: FRE Beat Driven by Record ACS Fees, Which Help Validate APO’s Origination Prowess and Offset a Modest Management Fee Miss, 20% FRE and 10% SRE Guide Maintained
reports.headline: APO 1Q26 FRE beat on record ACS fees; OW/$150 PT maintained; FY26 EPS trimmed, FY27 raised
reports.summary: JPM reiterates OW on APO with Dec-26 PT unchanged at $150 after 1Q26 ANI/sh of $1.94 beat cons $1.88, driven by FRE/PII strength offsetting an SRE miss. The key variant point is ACS fees staying above $200mm for a 4th straight qtr, supporting JPM’s view that APO’s origination engine is the strongest part of the story. Mgmt maintained CY26 guide for 20% FRE growth and 10% normalized SRE growth, while JPM modestly cuts FY26 EPS to $8.83 from $8.88 and raises FY27 EPS to $10.87 from $10.76.
important_observations: Record ACS fees of $246mm beat $185mm Street and drove FRE of $728mm vs $693mm cons despite modest mgmt fee miss and higher comp/opex.
SRE missed at $719mm vs $736mm cons due mainly to higher cost of funds; retirement net spread fell to 97bps from 120bps, but mgmt said ex idiosyncratic events it would be within 120-125bps FY guide.
APO originated $71bn in 1Q26, down q/q from $97bn but LTM originations rose to $324bn, already well above the $275bn 2029 investor-day target.
Fundraising remained broad-based with $50bn organic inflows plus $65bn from PIC; AMAPS added $5bn in qtr and has contributed ~$20bn since inception.
JPM is increasingly comfortable with a higher ACS base case, raising ACS fee ests materially for FY26/FY27.
Higher SBC weighs on FRE valuation contribution, limiting PT upside despite stronger FY27 ANI/sh.

reports.title: Apollo Global Management Inc. (APO): Strength in FRE partially offset by lingering SRE uncertainty
reports.headline: GS raises APO EPS/PT on stronger FRE, but flags lingering 2026 SRE guide risk.
reports.summary: GS reiterates Buy on APO and raises ests after 1Q26, with stronger FRE driven by mgmt fee growth, transaction fees, fundraising and origination, partly offset by concern that SRE needs a meaningful ramp to hit 2026 guide. GS models 25% FRE growth in 2026 vs mgmt's 20%+ guide, but only ~8% normalized SRE growth vs mgmt's 10% target, citing annuity competition, uneven FABN issuance and slower PRT. PT moves to $142 from $134 as ests rise and valuation rolls forward.
important_observations: FRE beat by 3% in 1Q on transaction fees and op lev; GS sees upside to mgmt's 20%+ 2026 FRE guide and models 25% growth.
SRE guide looks more exposed: 10% normalized growth implies ~$970mn/qtr vs $907mn normalized in 1Q despite spreads already at 122bps.
GS believes hitting SRE guide likely requires gross inflows above the $85bn+ target, given limited room for spread expansion.
Origination strength supports APO's moat in IG private credit; AI/digital infra, energy, manufacturing and defense highlighted as LT demand drivers.
Fundraising expected to accel in 2H26 with latest PE flagship, though wealth growth faces NT BDC headwinds.
new_data: 1Q organic inflows were $50bn: $30bn Asset Mgmt and $20bn Retirement Services.
1Q origination vols were $71bn; mgmt expects higher q/q origination in 2Q.
1Q SRE was $817mn actual and $907mn normalized, pressured by lower Alts returns related to MFS/AAA.
Retail annuities remained range-bound at ~$7bn amid heightened competition.
New markets, Stable Value and structured settlements, expected to generate >$5bn inflows in 2026 vs <$1bn in 2025.
management_insights: Mgmt reaffirmed 20%+ FRE growth in 2026, with high-teens comp expense growth and low-DD FRE non-comp expense growth.
Mgmt reaffirmed 10% normalized SRE growth in 2026 assuming 11% Alts portfolio marks.
Mgmt expects retail annuities to stay in the $7bn-$10bn range over next few qtrs and views FABN as episodic.
Mgmt cited increasingly robust institutional demand across Credit from more attractive spreads and dislocation.
Mgmt remains disciplined on underwriting and unwilling to overextend into unprofitable retirement flows.
estimate_changes: FRE ests raised ~3% on avg after stronger 1Q jumping-off point and better fundraising/origination commentary.
12-mo PT raised to $142 from $134; Buy reiterated.

Institutional engagement remains strong/accelerating, supporting the broader asset-gathering debate for large alt managers.

reports.title: "Credit Score Carousel" Call Takeaways
reports.headline: Mizuho call highlights VS4 eligibility but unresolved GSE/MBS implementation details, with score-shopping risk and possible upward pressure on G-fees.
reports.summary: Mizuho hosted an investor call with Vipul Jain, MD RMBS Strategy, focused on mortgage credit-score model changes. Main takeaways: agency MBS investors care most about prepmt risk while GSEs use credit scores for credit risk/pricing; VS4 and Classic FICO are correlated but not directly convertible, with VS4 roughly 15 pts higher on avg; and recent FHA/GSE actions confirm VS4 eligibility but leave key details unresolved, including LLPA grids, pooling rules, and commingled collateral treatment. For FICO, Mizuho remains OP and lifted PT to $1,494 from $1,416; TRU remains N at $80 and EFX OP at $222.
important_observations: VS4 running ~15 pts higher than Classic FICO creates potential score inflation if lenders select the more favorable score at the margin.
Direct VS4-to-FICO conversion is imperfect; Mizuho frames it as SAT vs ACT, not Celsius vs Fahrenheit, which complicates investor and lender modeling.
Agency MBS investors focus mainly on prepmt rather than credit loss due to GSE guarantees, but scores still matter for prepmt models and risk-based pricing.
Missing LLPA grids/pooling rules limit ability to map score differences into mortgage rates or adverse-selection risk.
Mizuho sees current uncertainty as potentially pressuring base G-fees, cited at ~55 bps.
new_data: VS4 scores run roughly ~15 pts higher on avg vs Classic FICO, per call discussion.
FHA and GSEs have made VS4 eligible, currently in limited rollout.

FICO PT raised to $1,494 from $1,416.

reports.title: Credit Model Wars—FICO vs VS4: Is Someone Selling Snake Oil, and if so, Who?
reports.headline: Jefferies argues FICO 10T superiority vs VS4 remains central to defending mortgage share; GSE internal tests favored 10T, but public 3rd-party testing awaits summer data release.
reports.summary: Jefferies frames the FICO vs VantageScore mortgage-score debate as noisy but highly relevant to FICO's ability to defend share, with competing commissioned studies reaching opposite conclusions: Prosperity Now says VS4 beats Classic FICO but did not test FICO 10T, while Milliman says FICO 10T outperforms VS4 across measures and mortgage types. The note emphasizes that lenders/MBS investors should ultimately favor the most predictive score because better predictive power should improve MBS pricing and consumer rates, but expects the market to remain unsettled until FICO 10T data becomes public for 3rd-party testing this summer.
important_observations: FICO mortgage-share defense hinges on lenders and MBS investors accepting 10T as the superior predictive score vs VS4.
Jefferies notes both GSEs preferred FICO 10T in internal testing, a key point supporting FICO's competitive argument.
Public debate is muddied by commissioned studies, methodological disputes, and careful wording from both FICO and VantageScore.
A more predictive score should theoretically receive better MBS pricing, creating lender incentive to use the score that lowers consumer rates.
Market consensus likely delayed until 10T data is publicly available and lenders complete their own testing.
new_data: Prosperity Now study dated May 5, 2026 concludes VS4 is superior to Classic FICO but does not benchmark FICO 10T.
Milliman study dated May 4, 2026 concludes FICO 10T is superior to VS4 across all measures and mortgage types.
FICO 10T data expected to become publicly available for 3rd-party testing this summer.

VantageScore mgmt disputed the Milliman study on methodology via press release.
Jefferies believes FICO mgmt would be unlikely to rely on 10T if it were not meaningfully superior to VS4.
Analyst challenges both mgmt teams to a technical PhD-level public panel to clarify model-performance claims.

reports.title: Carvana: Takeaways from Investor Group Call with Investor Relations Team
reports.headline: IR call frames 2Q retail GPU pressure as temporary industry spread issue; recon and finance execution improving, SG&A overhead likely flat NT.
reports.summary: JPM summarizes a CVNA investor group call focused on 1Q26 results and model drivers. Mgmt reiterated a $100-200/unit 2Q26 retail GPU headwind from industry-wide wholesale/retail spread compression, but expects normalization within ~60 days as high-cost inventory clears. Recon pressure should ease gradually with Apr productivity improvement, finance attach/APR competitiveness has improved, wholesale GPU likely normalizes from elevated 1Q levels, and NT overhead dollars should remain roughly flat.
important_observations: Wholesale/retail spread compression is framed as industry-wide and self-correcting, not CVNA-specific, with potential relief if retail prices catch up.
Retail GPU faces both 2Q26 spread pressure and non-repeat of a ~$100/unit y/y benefit from post-tariff pre-buy demand last year.
Recon headwinds were similar in 1Q26 vs 4Q25, but Apr productivity neared record labor-hour util/unit; GPU benefit should lag sell-through.
Wholesale GPU of ~$1.3K/unit in 1Q26 was above historical ~$1K range and likely reverts, though ADESA Clear and faster pickup-to-sale time may support upside.
Mgmt appears to have largely closed the APR gap vs peers for prime customers, supporting finance attach and slower prepay speeds.
Competitive dynamics described as unchanged; CVNA and KMX still too small relative to total used-car market for material mutual impact.
new_data: 2Q26 retail GPU headwind reiterated at $100-200/unit from wholesale/retail spread compression.
Industry normalization expected within ~60 days as elevated-cost inventory sells through.

1Q26 loan backlog build was ~$231mn, partly seasonal due to back-weighted tax-refund demand.
1Q26 overhead dollars were ~$201mn and likely flat NT; ad spend ~$630/retail unit viewed as run-rate NT.

management_insights: IR said wholesale/retail spread volatility reflected dealers stocking aggressively ahead of tax season while retail prices lagged and refunds were softer than expected.
Mgmt indicated recon tech/tools remain early-stage; NT efficiency gains are more from execution discipline than tech leverage.
Mgmt said strong residual-class demand in the Mar securitization reflected favorable credit performance and prepay experience, but sustainability needs time.

reports.title: Shop 'Til You Drop; Quick 10-Q Takes
reports.headline: PGR 10-Q shows continued heavy ad spend, limited rate action, softer PLE, and still-solid auto margins as competition/shopping intensify.
reports.summary: Wells Fargo’s quick 10-Q take on PGR highlights a more competitive personal auto market: rates were down less than 1%, frequency flat, severity +3%, and PLE still declining as shopping rises. PGR kept leaning into growth with $1.5b Q1 ad spend, +20% y/y, while ex-ad expense ratio improved modestly. Margins remain strong vs peers, but the debate is whether PIF growth has peaked as peers push for share; WF stays EW with a $218 PT.
important_observations: Competition/shopping is pressuring PLE and written premium per policy, especially in agency auto.
PGR is still investing aggressively in ads despite competitive pressure, suggesting mgmt is prioritizing growth/retention while margins allow.
Personal auto margin position remains strong vs peers, with Q1 underlying combined ratio near Geico and reserve releases supporting results.
Targeted rate decreases in profitable states/markets may accelerate growth but could narrow margin cushion if severity/frequency worsen.
Upcoming call focus expected on PIF growth durability, competitive intensity, ad spend, gas prices, supply chain disruption, and excess capital.

reports.title: Virtual NDR takeaways
reports.headline: DB NDR with FICO mgmt: pricing/value strategy intact despite faster VantageScore 4.0 developments
reports.summary: DB hosted FICO mgmt after UWM began accepting VantageScore 4.0, with mgmt arguing FICO's moat and price-for-value strategy remain intact, no volume loss is expected, and pricing model flexibility can protect total rev. DB sees Vantage developments moving faster than expected, which could either clear the stock overhang if FICO proves resilience or become the key downside risk if FICO loses meaningful share or the mortgage ecosystem treats FICO and VantageScore at parity.
important_observations: VantageScore 4.0 adoption is progressing faster than DB expected, especially with UWM already accepting it for conventional loans.
UWM's Vantage offering is limited to max 80% LTV and min 640 VantageScore, with a 20-pt haircut used to map to the current FICO Classic LLPA grid.
Mgmt argues UWM using the FICO pricing grid for Vantage suggests the industry lacks a separate robust Vantage pricing framework.
FICO sees dual-score usage as encouraging gaming and says score differences are large enough that a 20-pt adjustment is arbitrary.
Mgmt believes any VantageScore usage that touches origination should still trigger compensation for FICO IP if FICO scores are used at any point.
10T was priced at parity with VantageScore at $0.99 to drive adoption, with funding-fee economics intended to spread cost among downstream users.
FICO 10T data availability in the next few months is an important NT catalyst for third-party evaluation vs VantageScore 4.0.
2H guide framed as conservative, with mgmt cautious on mortgage vols and flagging potential DLP timing impact.
new_data: UWM announced it can accept VantageScore for conventional loan submissions.
UWM Vantage loans capped at 80% LTV and require min 640 VantageScore.
UWM applies a 20-pt reduction to VantageScore for pricing off the FICO Classic LLPA grid.
FICO says 30% of the time Vantage and FICO differ by 20 pts in either direction.
Mgmt estimates only a small fraction of consumers would get better pricing with Vantage; illustrative math implies 9% of total mortgages could theoretically price better via Vantage.
60% of conforming mortgages are below 80% LTV.
55 mortgage lenders are testing FICO 10T; if in full production, they would originate approx $495B in non-conforming market volume, equivalent to approx $1.6T servicing.
F2Q mortgage rev grew +127% y/y, driven mainly by solid vol with no one-time items.
management_insights: Mgmt continues to see a significant gap between FICO score price and value.
Mgmt's priority is optimizing total rev rather than unit cost, with multiple pricing scenarios under review.
Mgmt does not expect volume loss from VantageScore adoption and views theoretical Vantage usage as incremental to industry volume, not a FICO loss.
Mgmt said FICO has flexibility to change its pricing model to ensure it is paid for IP usage.
Mgmt said 10T architecture is similar to FICO Classic, lowering operational lift for mortgage ecosystem adoption.
Mgmt is waiting for FHFA/GSE sign-off on DLP and expects significant pent-up lender demand from cost savings.
Software Platform ARR accel reflects strong ACV bookings over the last few qtrs, helped by broader AI narrative.
Mgmt noted modest auto price increase, similar to last year.
estimate_changes: No rev/EPS estimate changes disclosed in the note.
DB rating remains Buy and current PT is $1,658.
Historical rec chart shows the most recent PT change occurred on Apr 15, 2026, before this report.

reports.title: Allstate Bull/Bear: Differing View Points Evolving
reports.headline: Post-1Q26 beat, bulls focus on strong auto CR/PYD and EPS durability while bears see early signs of auto margin/NWP deterioration into 2027.
reports.summary: MS frames ALL’s 1Q26 as a divided bull/bear setup: headline EPS and auto CR were strong, helped by favorable PYD and solid PIF growth, but underlying AYLR ex-CAT worsened and auto NWP growth was flat despite higher units. MS remains cautious on personal auto over-earning, expecting pricing decel and loss-cost pressure to drive EPS compression from 2026e to 2027e, with soft-market risk potentially extending into 2028+.
important_observations: Auto CR beat was substantial, but quality is debated because much of the strength came from favorable PYD rather than AY underwriting.
Bears view 68.5% auto AYLR ex-CAT as an early red flag that loss-cost trend and competition are starting to pressure margins.
Flat auto NWP growth despite higher PIF suggests premium per policy pressure and potential pricing softness.
MS sees ALL’s premium growth profile as better than several peers, reducing NT impact from AYLR deterioration but not eliminating the 2027 EPS compression risk.
Normalized valuation looks fuller than headline P/E implies; MS estimates ALL trades at ~11.7x NTM P/E under a 96% normalized Auto/Homeowners CR framework.
new_data: 1Q26 auto CR was 81.9% vs cons 90.8% and 91.3% in 1Q25.
1Q26 auto AYLR ex-CAT was 68.5%, worse than expected.
Favorable PYD ratio was 8.8%, or ~$838mm favorable.
Auto NWP growth was 0% y/y in 1Q26 despite higher PIF count.
Industry and ALL auto rate filings have moved negative in recent months, pointing to further pricing deterioration.
BLS auto severity components remain elevated in recent months.

MS expects EPS compression from 2026e to 2027e as auto pricing decel and loss-cost trends pressure peak margins.

reports.title: TSMC: April sales in line; 2Q26 likely at high end of guidance amid tight leading-edge capacity
reports.headline: Apr sales in line; 2Q26 rev likely high end of guide as N5/N3 tightness supports GM and pricing into 2027.
reports.summary: JPM says TSMC’s Apr sales were on track and 2Q26 should land at the high end of guide, driven by GPU/ASIC, CPU networking, Apple iPhone/Mac, agentic AI and server CPU demand. The note argues leading-edge and CoWoS capacity tightness should persist into 2027 despite aggressive capex, supporting mid/high-60s GM, 2027 N3/N2 price hikes, and continued >95% TSMC share in leading-edge nodes despite rising INTC/Samsung customer engagement noise.

N3 2026 demand-supply gap estimated at ~600k wafers, equal to US$15-18bn foundry rev, even with N3 util at 140-145%.
JPM expects 2027 pricing talks to start in 2Q26, with ~7-10% N3 hike and 3-5% N2 hike; no N5 price increase expected.
CoWoS supply/demand gap estimated at 15-20%; outsourcing to OSATs such as ASE and VSMC interposer/silicon bridge work should rise but not close shortage before 2027.
Customer trials at INTC/Samsung seen as shortage-driven noise, not share loss thesis; JPM expects TSMC to retain at least 95% leading-edge share.

reports.title: LVMH: The self-help moment; upgrade to OW
reports.headline: Barclays upgs LVMH to OW, arguing Tiffany/Dior self-help can drive accel from 2Q26 and MT growth above sector while valuation is at a hist discount.
reports.summary: Barclays upgs MC FP to OW from EW and raises PT to EUR600 from EUR570 after a 26% YTD derating, seeing an attractive setup into a 2Q26 growth inflection. The call is built on sector growth stabilizing at c4%, but MC delivering c5.4% FY27-29 rev CAGR via Tiffany renovations/assortment elevation, Dior creative reset, Sephora expansion, and mix-led margin recovery. FY26 EPS is slightly cut, but FY27-28 EPS sits 2-3% above cons and valuation at c20x 12m fwd P/E is a 16% discount to hist avg.

W&J is the main variant-perception upside: FY27-29e CAGR of 8.4%, c150 bps above cons, led by Tiffany reaching cEUR7bn sales by 2029e.
Dior is expected to return to its 2023 peak rev of cEUR8.9bn by 2029e after creative reset, despite Barclays staying slightly below cons on FLG.
Valuation support is central: c20x 12m fwd P/E, 16% below hist avg, leaves MC among cheapest in Barclays luxury coverage except Prada.

reports.summary: BofA keeps INTC at Underperform despite raising PO to $96 from $56, arguing the potential Apple foundry deal and higher server CPU TAM are already reflected in valuation while AMD/ARM remain better positioned share gainers. reports.title: Q1: Opening Up for Bigger Opportunities; Reiterate Top Pick
reports.headline: APP Q1 beat, Q2 guide better than seasonal, Consumer/e-comm traction boosts 2H26 confidence; Buy, $700 PT maintained.
reports.summary: Jefferies reiterates APP as a Top Pick after Q1 rev grew 59% y/y and 11% q/q, above guide, with Q2 guide implying 4-6% q/q growth in a seasonally weak qtr. The main incremental positive is Consumer/e-comm: April hit a monthly ad-spend record ahead of full GA self-serve launch in Jun, and avg new paid-marketing customer is projected to spend >$70K/yr. Jefferies raises ests modestly, remains above cons, and keeps Buy/$700 PT on confidence in 2H26 rev reaccel and FY27 cons conservatism.
important_observations: Q1 sequential rev growth of 11% was strong given Q1 is typically weak for e-comm ads.
Q2 guide implies 4-6% q/q growth into APP's historically weakest qtr and may support 2-yr stack accel for first time since Q4'24.
Consumer/e-comm April monthly record came before full GA launch, suggesting customer growth and model improvements are improving advertiser performance.
Jefferies sees potential 2H26 rev reaccel to 57% in Q3 and 58% in Q4 if APP delivers typical upside vs guide.
Key variant debate is whether investors can underwrite accelerating 2H26 rev and view Street FY27 rev growth of 30% as conservative.

reports.title: Building Momentum in Ecommerce; Reiterate OW
reports.headline: APP 1Q beat, web/ecomm momentum, gaming strength; MS reits OW/$720 PT and raises '26/'27 EBITDA 3%.
reports.summary: MS says APP delivered another strong qtr, with 1Q rev/EBITDA 4%/6% above prior ests and ad rev +11% q/q vs co target of +6%, driven by gaming model gains and late-qtr web/ecomm improvements. Mgmt commentary pointed to stronger web exit rates, record Apr web rev, immediate ROAS gains from recent model upgrades, and June self-serve as a key catalyst. MS raises '26/'27 rev, EPS and EBITDA modestly, keeps OW and $720 PT, arguing APP remains the blue-chip mobile ad-tech name despite sector multiple pressure.
important_observations: Web/ecomm appears to be accelerating 18 months post-launch, with Apr setting a monthly web rev record and mgmt citing recent model improvements that drove advertiser budget increases.
MS estimates web contributed ~$350mn of 1Q rev and still expects ~$1.8bn of non-gaming ad rev in '26, rising above $3bn by '28.
Gaming ads remain stronger than expected, with estimated rev +44% y/y and +97% on a 2-yr stack; q/q growth has exceeded 5% for 13 straight qtrs.
Bull case rests on conversion-rate upside: current in-game conversion is cited at ~1.3%, leaving room for tech-driven gains in high-ad-load environments.
Self-serve launch in Jun and expansion of non-endemic ad inventory into more games are key NT catalysts.

reports.title: Carvana: Checks the Right Boxes in Q1; Share Gains Robust & LT Opportunity Remains Compelling
reports.headline: Q1 units/Adj EBITDA beat, share gains remain robust, Wells raises FY26/27 EPS and PT to $475.
reports.summary: Wells sees CVNA higher post-Q1 as retail units and Adj EBITDA beat Street by ~3%/~4%, Q2 unit guide implies continued strong growth, and initiatives around capacity, reconditioning, and cost efficiency appear to be working. NT consumer/credit/gas risks remain, but the note argues LT share gains and capacity unlock support a compelling setup; Wells keeps OW and raises PT to $475 from $425.
important_observations: Q1 weekly units rose +15% q/q to ~14.4K; Q2 guide for sequential improvement implies 31%+ y/y unit growth, while Wells models +35%.
CVNA Q1 retail unit growth/Adj EBITDA margin of +40%/~10.4% materially outpaced avg traditional used auto dealers at -2%/~5%.
Website units grew +31% y/y in Q1 but declined -7% q/q, a watch item despite overall unit momentum.

Retail GPU improved sequentially but was down y/y; Q2 retail GPU expected to improve q/q but decline y/y due to tougher comps, lower shipping fees, higher non-vehicle costs, and narrower wholesale/retail spreads.
16 ADESA site integrations complete through Q1; CVNA still expects 6-8 more in 2026 with full build-out construction starting during the year.
